rationale: Medicine is the highest-volume level-1 (16% of all papers) and has the most established subspecialty structure of any category — clinicians literally introduce themselves by their subspecialty. I used the boarded-specialty list as the spine: organ-system-based fields (Cardiology, Gastroenterology), patient-population fields (Pediatrics, Geriatrics), and modality-based fields (Surgery, Radiology, Anesthesia, Pathology). I kept Surgery as ONE card despite being split into 8+ sub-specialties in MeSH — the picker can't carry that, and a surgeon's level-2 identity is 'I'm a surgeon' before 'I'm a colorectal surgeon'. I split Internal Medicine into its boarded subspecialties (Cardiology, Pulmonology, Gastroenterology, Endocrinology, Rheumatology, Hematology, Nephrology, Infectious Disease, Oncology) because each has dedicated journals and conferences and practitioners identify with the subspecialty, not 'Internal Medicine'. Dermatology gets its own card (yes captain — but at the same level as other organ-system specialties, no special treatment). Oncology straddles internal medicine and surgery — I split it out because cancer has the most distinct paper ecosystem in all of medicine.

subspecialties.name: Cardiology
covers: coronary disease, heart failure, arrhythmia, valve disease, structural and interventional cardiology, electrophysiology
JACC, Circulation, EHJ

subspecialties.name: Oncology
covers: medical oncology, radiation oncology, surgical oncology, hematologic malignancies, solid tumors, immunotherapy, targeted therapy
JCO, Nat Cancer, Lancet Oncol, Cancer Cell
explicitly_excludes: cancer cell biology basic science → Biology

subspecialties.name: Neurology
covers: stroke, epilepsy, MS, Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, ALS, neuromuscular disease, headache, sleep medicine
Neurology, Lancet Neurol, Ann Neurol, Brain

subspecialties.name: Psychiatry
covers: mood disorders, schizophrenia, anxiety, addiction medicine, psychopharmacology, geriatric psychiatry, child psychiatry
Am J Psychiatry, Lancet Psychiatry, JAMA Psychiatry
explicitly_excludes: cognitive science basic research → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Dermatology
subspecialties.tagline: Skin, hair, nails — medical and surgical.
covers: inflammatory skin disease, dermato-oncology, dermatopathology, pediatric dermatology, aesthetic and procedural dermatology, dermatosurgery
JAAD, Br J Dermatol, JID, JEADV

subspecialties.name: Gastroenterology & Hepatology
covers: IBD, GI motility, hepatology, NAFLD, pancreatic disease, endoscopy, GI cancers as clinical management
Gastroenterology, Gut, Hepatology
explicitly_excludes: GI surgery as primary topic → Surgery

subspecialties.name: Pulmonology & Critical Care
covers: COPD, asthma, ILD, ARDS, critical care medicine, sleep-disordered breathing, pulmonary hypertension
AJRCCM, CHEST, ERJ, Thorax

subspecialties.name: Endocrinology & Diabetes
covers: diabetes (T1, T2), obesity medicine, thyroid disease, adrenal, pituitary, bone metabolism, reproductive endocrinology
Diabetes Care, JCEM, Lancet D&E

subspecialties.name: Nephrology
covers: AKI, CKD, dialysis, kidney transplant, glomerular disease, electrolyte disorders, hypertension management
JASN, KI, Am J Kid Dis, NDT
explicitly_excludes: kidney transplant surgery as surgical technique → Surgery

subspecialties.name: Hematology
covers: benign hematology (anemias, hemoglobinopathies), coagulation, transfusion medicine, bone marrow failure, hematologic malignancies overlap
Blood, Haematologica, Leukemia

subspecialties.name: Rheumatology & Clinical Immunology
covers: RA, SLE, vasculitis, spondyloarthropathies, allergic disease, primary immunodeficiency, scleroderma
ARD, A&R, Lancet Rheum

subspecialties.name: Infectious Disease
subspecialties.tagline: Bacterial, viral, fungal, antimicrobial stewardship, ID hospital medicine.
covers: HIV, TB, hepatitis, hospital-acquired infections, AMR, sepsis management, tropical medicine, vaccine clinical trials
CID, Lancet Infect Dis, JID
explicitly_excludes: epidemiology and outbreak modeling → Public Health

subspecialties.name: Obstetrics & Gynecology
covers: obstetrics, maternal-fetal medicine, gynecologic oncology overlap, reproductive endocrinology, fertility, contraception, menopause
Am J Obstet Gynecol, BJOG, Obstet Gynecol
explicitly_excludes: GYN surgery as surgical technique → Surgery

subspecialties.name: Pediatrics
covers: general pediatrics, pediatric subspecialties (when child-specific is the angle), neonatology, adolescent medicine, developmental-behavioral pediatrics
Pediatrics, J Pediatr, Lancet Child Adol Health
explicitly_excludes: pediatric cancer in adult context → Oncology

subspecialties.name: Geriatrics & Palliative Care
covers: geriatric medicine, frailty, polypharmacy, palliative care, hospice medicine, dementia care management
explicitly_excludes: cognitive decline neuroscience → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

subspecialties.name: Primary Care & Family Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: General internal medicine, family practice, ambulatory care.
covers: family medicine, general internal medicine, ambulatory care, preventive medicine in clinic
Ann Intern Med, JAMA Intern Med, BMJ, Ann Fam Med

subspecialties.name: Surgery
covers: general, vascular, cardiothoracic, neurosurgery, orthopedics, plastic, transplant, trauma, urology, ENT, ophthalmic surgery, surgical oncology technique
Ann Surg, JAMA Surg, NEJM surgical content
explicitly_excludes: medical management of surgical conditions → that organ-system specialty

subspecialties.name: Anesthesiology & Pain Medicine
covers: general and regional anesthesia, perioperative medicine, pain medicine (acute, chronic, interventional), critical care anesthesia
explicitly_excludes: critical care from pulmonology angle → Pulmonology & Critical Care

subspecialties.name: Radiology & Imaging
covers: diagnostic radiology, interventional radiology, nuclear medicine, MR/CT physics-as-clinical, AI for imaging clinical validation
Radiology, AJR, Eur Radiol, JNM
explicitly_excludes: computer vision methods → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Pathology & Laboratory Medicine
subspecialties.tagline: Diagnostic pathology, molecular path, lab medicine.
covers: anatomic and clinical pathology, molecular diagnostics, lab medicine, cytopathology, hematopathology, digital pathology
Mod Pathol, Am J Surg Pathol, J Clin Pathol

subspecialties.name: Emergency Medicine
covers: emergency care, resuscitation, toxicology, prehospital and EMS, disaster medicine
Ann Emerg Med, Acad Emerg Med, AJEM

subspecialties.name: Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation
subspecialties.tagline: Rehab, sports medicine, spine, MSK medicine.
covers: PM&R, rehabilitation medicine, sports medicine, spine medicine (non-surgical), musculoskeletal medicine, prosthetics and orthotics

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Hematologic Oncology from Hematology — they overlap heavily and most practitioners are dual-trained. Kept under Hematology with Oncology overlap noted.
Considered separating Sleep Medicine from Pulmonology — but most sleep papers are pulmonary or neuro and assigning them properly happens at level 3.

Considered separating Ophthalmology and ENT (Otolaryngology) — they ARE separate residencies. But in the paper-volume ranking they're each <1.5% of medicine and would crowd the picker. Folded into Surgery as a temporary compromise; can split in v2 if user feedback complains.
Considered Genomic / Precision Medicine — it's a method, not a specialty; lands in Oncology, Cardiology, etc. as appropriate at level 3.
Veterinary Medicine: explicitly excluded — folds under Agriculture & Food at level 1.
